resources.string: Open the native app language settings for i18n testing without changing the system language.
Android native per-app language settings; falls back to the app info page when the direct page is unavailable.
Unable to open app language settings. Open this app in system settings and choose Language.
Keep the current rhythm for page switches, search bars, sheets, and expand transitions.
Use direct show/hide transitions to reduce extra animation cost on weaker devices.
Affects main page switching, search bars, bottom bar visibility, expandable areas, and common capsule press transitions.
System back gestures preview the previous page while dragging, making hierarchy clearer.
Back gestures return directly, reducing visual distraction from page previews.
Affects system back gesture previews on Settings, About, Guide, and other navigation-stack pages.
Theme mode, preload behavior, and Home highlight options.
Manage page transitions and interaction motion.
Manage ActionBar, bottom bar, and card feedback visuals.
Manage non-Home background image switches, assets, and opacity presets.
Manage Super Island templates, Live Update, and HyperOS compatibility bypass behavior.
Manage lightweight copy and full selection modes.
Capsule bottom bar, selected highlight, and lightweight glass texture
Keep the full Liquid Glass look while lists or pages scroll, for steadier bottom-bar interaction.
Use lightweight bottom-bar effects during scrolling or paging to prioritize weaker devices.
Affects the main pager, Settings, Guide catalog, and student-detail bottom bars during scroll.
Use the layered liquid style: capsule base, sliding highlight, and selected-state motion.
Keep the sliding highlight only. Release triggers highlight buttons; direct taps run immediately.
Top ActionBar on Home / OS / MCP / GitHub / BA / Guide pages
Use v2 Liquid Glass switches, buttons, and capsule controls for consistent liquid interaction.
Use solid capsule fallback and the Miuix switch style for consistent low-load visuals.
Affects Settings, sheets, business-page button groups, and page configuration controls.
Enable all cards that support PressFeedback.
Disable Sink / Tilt and other card press feedback globally.
System / MCP / GitHub / BA and other cards that support press feedback
Preload adjacent pages and guide resources for faster common page switches and returns.
Use the current lower-cost strategy to prioritize memory and burst-load control.
Affects main pager preloading, guide-page preloading, and static guide media prefetch.
Home icon and title HDR highlight
Enable linked highlight effects for the Kei icon and KeiOS title. The effect is brighter with the screen on.
Disabled by default. Enabling this can trigger HDR highlight output.
Affects the linked highlight on the Home Kei icon and KeiOS title. Enabling this can trigger HDR.
Keep full dynamic background and glass strength while scrolling Home, paging, and tapping.
Use the regular Home dynamic strategy during motion to control burst rendering cost.
Affects the Home dynamic background, Home overview-card glass, and Home top ActionBar effects during motion.
Non-Home pages show a custom background image while keeping the current layout and layering.
Non-Home pages use the default solid background.
Affects OS / MCP / GitHub / BA and other non-Home main pages.
Background image set. It applies when entering non-Home pages.
Current opacity: %1$d%%. Use preset stops for quick switching.
Recommended default: %1$d%%, balancing readability and atmosphere.
Compatibility bypass enabled (high risk): temporarily changes system network rules to trigger Super Island.
Disabled by default (recommended): reduces HyperOS status bar and System UI risk.
Applies only to the Super Island style. Live Update does not use this bypass.
Wait %1$dms after sending, then restore `com.xiaomi.xmsf` network access.
Only affects restore timing after enabling Super Island compatibility bypass. MCP / GitHub / BA Super Island notifications share this delay.
Recommended default: %1$dms. 8 Elite Gen5 can try 75ms; other devices should start from the default.
Use the Super Island template (Hyper Focus). Turn off to use AOSP Live Update notifications.
Currently using AOSP Live Update notifications. Turn on to restore the Super Island template.
Affects the visual style of MCP keepalive notifications and BA AP notifications.
This app is allowed to ignore battery optimization, improving MCP keepalive and background notifications.
System battery optimization is active, so background keepalive and real-time notifications may be reclaimed more often.
Unable to open battery optimization settings. Adjust it manually in system settings.
%1$s allows app auto-start. Keepalive services and notifications can recover more reliably after boot.
%1$s restricts app auto-start. Service recovery and background reminders may be delayed after boot.
%1$s provides an auto-start management page. Current status cannot be read; open it to check manually.
This device has no stable OEM shortcut. The button opens this app in system app info for permission and background management.
This device has no separate OEM auto-start permission page. The app follows the system default startup policy.
No separate entry on this device
Unable to open OEM auto-start settings. Check the system security app manually.
Notifications are allowed, so live reminders, background notices, and keepalive status can display reliably.
Notification permission is restricted, so MCP status, background reminders, and keepalive notifications may fail to display.
Unable to open notification settings. Adjust it manually in system settings.
Currently preferring Shizuku mode for app-list reading, covering a wider installed-package scope.
Currently using direct system app-list reading, suitable for normal local app matching.
App-list access is limited, so imported or matched local apps may have a narrower visible scope.
Unable to open app-list related settings. Check permissions on the app info page manually.
Shizuku is connected. App-list detection and some system capabilities prefer the Shizuku channel.
Shizuku authorization is unavailable. Related advanced capabilities fall back to direct system mode.
Full copy mode: text supports long-press selection, drag selection, select all, and the system text menu.
Lightweight copy mode: long-press an item to copy “title: value”, with lower scroll and interaction cost.
Enables system text selection for text rows. This mode is more complete and touch-heavy; use it as needed for long lists.
Uses lightweight copy everywhere for smoother scrolling. Temporarily enable this option when drag selection or select-all is needed.
Count cached items, storage size, and recent activity across pages.
Skip cache directory and storage traversal to reduce extra Settings-page cost.
Settings reads cache summaries from GitHub / MCP / System / BA and other pages.
Only disables Settings-page cache diagnostics. Page caches and normal usage remain active.
Cache diagnostics are disabled. Settings will skip cache size, update time, and cleanup stats.
Cross-page cache and configuration usage overview.
Cache estimate %1$s · Config estimate %2$s · MMKV used %3$s · Icon memory %4$s
Cache estimate %1$s · Config estimate %2$s · MMKV used %3$s · Media disk %4$s
Cache estimate %1$s · Config estimate %2$s · MMKV used %3$s
Cache estimate %1$s · Config estimate %2$s · Disk %3$s · Scanned %4$d items · Hits %5$d items/%6$d spans/%7$s
Cache estimate %1$s · Config estimate %2$s · Disk %3$s · Directory %4$s
Version check results and GitHub tracking config.
Tracked %1$d items · Check cache %2$d items · Asset cache %3$d items · %4$s
Event schedule, recruitment cache, and page state.
Server %1$d · Schedule cache %2$s · Recruitment cache %3$s · Images %4$d
Student guide details and archive catalog cache.
Detail cache %1$d items · Catalog Released Students %2$d / NPC & Upcoming %3$d
In-memory icon cache for GitHub pages and related lists.
Temporary images and media files for student guides.
ExoPlayer cache for voice lines and media playback.
Files %1$d · Cleanup runs %2$d · Recent hits %3$d · Recent freed %4$s
Redirected output directory for uiautomator dumps.
Local persisted items such as service name, port, and auth token.
Record app debug logs as needed, with size view, cleanup, and ZIP export.
Verbose logging enabled: network, notifications, MCP, performance, and related flows write local log files.
Verbose logging disabled: the app runs with lower overhead. Existing logs can be exported or cleared manually.
Affects the unified AppLogger flow. Debug builds enable this by default; release builds disable it by default.
Enable before reproducing an issue, then export the ZIP afterward. Logs keep growing, so clear them after diagnosis.
Keep disabled for daily use; enable temporarily while diagnosing issues.
